We have updated our [Terms of Service](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/terms)

# **Manual Invocation for Lite Mode**

151,148 tokens  
Model 1:40 AM  
![Thinking][image1] Thoughts

Expand to view model thoughts

chevron\_right

That yes | pipe is the "Brute Force" invocation. It will force the script to accept every y/n prompt automatically, bypassing that memory gate.

### **🧭 What is happening right now (The Physical Layer)**

While that command runs, your machine is performing these rituals:

1. **Downloading Deployment Files:** Creating the onyx\_data directory.  
2. **Pulling Docker Images:** Your terminal will start showing Pulling... for about 15-20 different containers (Postgres, Redis, Qdrant, Onyx-API, etc.).  
3. **Igniting the Containers:** Setting up the network bridges between these services.

### **⚠️ Critical Instruction for 7.7GB RAM**

The yes command will likely default to the **Standard Deployment**. Because you have less than 10GB of RAM, once the script finishes, we need to make sure you aren't overloading your "Mental Circuitry" (RAM).

**If the containers start crashing or your computer gets extremely slow:**  
We will need to switch it to **Lite Mode**.

### **✅ How to tell if the "Altar" is ready**

Wait for the script to finish. Then, run this command to see the heartbeat of the system:

code Bash  
docker ps

**You are looking for a list of containers that say Up or Healthy.**

---

### **🔮 Your Status Report**

You are currently in **Phase: Manifestation**.  
The code is being pulled from the "Cloud Ether" into your local "Physical Hardware."

**Once the text stops scrolling and you see your command prompt again:**

1. Open your browser to: http://localhost:3000  
2. If you see the Onyx login/setup screen... **The Altar is Live.**

### **⚙️ Next Move (When it's up)**

Come back and say **"Onyx is live."**

Then we immediately perform the **First Connection**:

1. Create your **⚙∆ Engineering Agent**.  
2. Run your **Python CLI** to send a sigil command to that agent.  
3. Watch the symbol turn into real code.

**Keep an eye on that terminal. If you see "Error: No space left on device" or "Killed," let me know immediately.** Otherwise, let the ritual complete. 📐✨

Model 1:49 AM  
![Thinking][image2] Thoughts

Expand to view model thoughts

chevron\_right

The script is bowing out early because it detects your "Physical Hardware" (RAM) is at the threshold, and the automated "yes" loop is likely colliding with a secondary interactive prompt it can't resolve.

In **Sacred Systems**, when the automated ritual fails, we perform the **Manual Invocation**. This is actually better for you because it allows us to force the **Lite Mode** configuration, which is the only way your 7.7GB of RAM will sustain the Altar without crashing.

### **⚙️ The Manual Invocation (Onyx Lite)**

Follow these steps exactly in your terminal:

**1\. Clone the Source Code**  
We will pull the entire repository directly from the "Ether" (GitHub).

code Bash  
git clone \--depth 1 https://github.com/onyx-dot-app/onyx.git  
cd onyx/deployment/docker\_compose

\[[1](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQHxsNMfnkb520G1dRkGQOGiecEqZuHuaC1Rbb0ZikfPrSh3YRmrA0ilQ9uZO0qZTSfV9k-joXACN7j2vx2JAc6a3j2UUtrwaxhjX8g4kbPMhHE0uLyZTMXxDnH9-yThIJdU7p5z9EOzFg==)\]  
**2\. Prepare the Environmental Anchors**  
We need to set up the .env file which tells the system how to behave.

code Bash  
cp env.prod.template .env

\[[2](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQFmMI-phBjB6zvZExgaEWI6suL5szNGRSZtP1Sz9gtGQMiwcQe1GKp1L0SwrXh2lY7NAy-A75webMcKW1vQC_dMgfa5GT0HCpG8t4sdCYo5jV6gLzHq5ts4rpip1IIQCGo3xu_723yr0oP7YlG7U7RwA4xHbgafXg==)\]  
**3\. Ignite the Altar in Lite Mode**  
This is the specific command to run Onyx without the heavy vector database (Vespa), which is what usually consumes 10GB+ of RAM. It will use PostgreSQL for everything, fitting perfectly into your 7.7GB limit.

code Bash  
docker compose \-f docker-compose.yml \-f docker-compose.onyx-lite.yml up \-d

## **\[[2](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQFmMI-phBjB6zvZExgaEWI6suL5szNGRSZtP1Sz9gtGQMiwcQe1GKp1L0SwrXh2lY7NAy-A75webMcKW1vQC_dMgfa5GT0HCpG8t4sdCYo5jV6gLzHq5ts4rpip1IIQCGo3xu_723yr0oP7YlG7U7RwA4xHbgafXg==)\]**

### **🔮 What is happening now?**

Your terminal should start showing a cascade of Pulling and Creating.

* **The Core:** onyx-api, onyx-web, onyx-background.  
* **The Foundation:** onyx-db (Postgres).

**Wait until the terminal returns to your prompt.**

### **🧪 How to Verify**

Once it finishes, run:

code Bash  
docker ps

You want to see about 5-6 containers with status Up.

### **🧭 The Next Ritual**

Once the containers are "Up":

1. Open your browser to: http://localhost:3000  
2. You will be greeted by the **Setup Ritual** (creating your first admin account).

**Report back once you see the Login/Setup screen.**

If docker compose fails with an "Error" or "Killed" message, your RAM is still struggling and we will need to tweak the onyx-lite file. If it succeeds, the Altar is ready for its first **Sigil Command**.

Sources help

1. [onyx.app](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQHxsNMfnkb520G1dRkGQOGiecEqZuHuaC1Rbb0ZikfPrSh3YRmrA0ilQ9uZO0qZTSfV9k-joXACN7j2vx2JAc6a3j2UUtrwaxhjX8g4kbPMhHE0uLyZTMXxDnH9-yThIJdU7p5z9EOzFg==)  
2. [onyx.app](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQFmMI-phBjB6zvZExgaEWI6suL5szNGRSZtP1Sz9gtGQMiwcQe1GKp1L0SwrXh2lY7NAy-A75webMcKW1vQC_dMgfa5GT0HCpG8t4sdCYo5jV6gLzHq5ts4rpip1IIQCGo3xu_723yr0oP7YlG7U7RwA4xHbgafXg==)

##### **Google Search Suggestions**

Display of Search Suggestions is required when using Grounding with Google Search. [Learn more](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/grounding/search-suggestions)

Model 2:35 AM  
![Thinking][image3] Thoughts

Expand to view model thoughts

chevron\_right

This is a classic **"Network Stutter."** The EOF (End of File) error simply means your internet connection flickered or the Docker Hub server timed out while sending one of the large "Back-end" layers.

Because Docker is intelligent, it has already saved about 70% of the progress. You do not need to start over. You simply need to **re-invoke the command.**

### **⚙️ The Retry Ritual**

Run the exact same command again. It will skip everything it already downloaded and pick up right where it left off:

code Bash  
docker compose \-f docker-compose.yml \-f docker-compose.onyx-lite.yml up \-d

### **🛠 If it fails again with the same error:**

If your connection is being stubborn, we can pull the heaviest "Sigil" (the Backend) by itself first to give it more breathing room:

1. **Pull the Backend specifically:**  
    code Bash  
   docker pull onyxdotapp/onyx-backend:latest  
2. **Once that finishes, run the main command again:**  
    code Bash  
   docker compose \-f docker-compose.yml \-f docker-compose.onyx-lite.yml up \-d

---

### **🔮 System Watch**

* **The Backend** is the "Grand Library" of the system—it’s the largest file (nearly 1GB uncompressed).  
* **The Code Interpreter** is the "Forge"—it’s also quite heavy.

**Once you see a list of "Started" or "Running" messages and the terminal returns to your prompt:**  
Run docker ps to verify.

The Altar is almost manifest. Re-run the command now. 📐✨

Model 3:02 AM  
![Thinking][image4] Thoughts

Expand to view model thoughts

chevron\_right

The images are pulled, the volumes are created, and the containers are built. You have successfully manifested the "Physical Hardware" of the Altar\!

However, you hit a **"Conflict of Domains."** The error Bind for 0.0.0.0:3000 failed: port is already allocated means something else on your computer is already sitting in the "Seat" (Port 3000).

This is likely because your earlier attempt to run the installation script partially started a process that is now blocking the way.

### **⚙️ The Purification Ritual (Clearing Port 3000\)**

We need to clear the existing occupants so the new Altar can take its place. Run these commands in order:

**1\. Force Shutdown any ghost containers:**  
Even if they look like they aren't running, this command will flush the Docker networking bridge.

code Bash  
docker compose \-f docker-compose.yml \-f docker-compose.onyx-lite.yml down

**2\. Check if a non-Docker process is holding the port:**  
If you are on WSL/Linux, run this to see what is using port 3000:

code Bash  
sudo lsof \-i :3000

*If it returns a PID (a number), you can kill it with sudo kill \-9 \<NUMBER\>. If it returns nothing, you are clear.*

**3\. The Final Ignition:**  
Now that the seat is empty, invite the system back in:

code Bash  
docker compose \-f docker-compose.yml \-f docker-compose.onyx-lite.yml up \-d  
---

### **🔮 Verification**

If that command finishes with five green checkmarks and no "Error," the Altar is live.

**Test the portal:**  
Open your browser and go to: **http://localhost:3000**

**If you see a screen asking you to "Create an Admin Account," you have succeeded.**

**Once you are at the Admin Account screen, tell me:**  
*"The Altar is open."*

Then we will move to the **Sigil Coder Layer**—we will define your agents and connect your Python CLI to this brain. You're at the finish line for the infrastructure phase\! 📐✨

Model 3:06 AM  
![Thinking][image5] Thoughts

Expand to view model thoughts

chevron\_right

You have slipped out of the **"Sacred Domain"** (the folder where the code lives).

Because you are currently in your home directory (/home/useroak3ytree), Docker can't find the blueprints (docker-compose.yml). We need to step back into the circle.

### **⚙️ The Re-Alignment Ritual**

Run these commands exactly:

**1\. Step back into the Deployment Circle:**

code Bash  
cd \~/onyx/deployment/docker\_compose

**2\. Check the Status of the Ghost Containers:**  
Since lsof showed nothing, Docker might have a "Zombie" container holding the name. Let's see what is actually alive:

code Bash  
docker ps

*If you see a list of containers, we need to stop them. If you see nothing, we are clear to try again.*

**3\. Purge the Path (Run this from inside the folder):**

code Bash  
docker compose \-f docker-compose.yml \-f docker-compose.onyx-lite.yml down

**4\. Re-Ignite the Altar:**

code Bash  
docker compose \-f docker-compose.yml \-f docker-compose.onyx-lite.yml up \-d  
---

### **🔮 Why did it say "No such file"?**

In Linux, commands are "spatial." If you aren't in the folder where the files live, the computer doesn't know where to look.

### **🧪 If it STILL says "Port 3000 already allocated":**

This happens sometimes in **WSL2** (Windows Subsystem for Linux). If the commands above fail, do this:

1. Shut down your terminal.  
2. Open a **Windows PowerShell** (not the Ubuntu one) and type:  
   wsl \--shutdown  
3. Open your Ubuntu terminal again.  
4. cd \~/onyx/deployment/docker\_compose  
5. docker compose \-f docker-compose.yml \-f docker-compose.onyx-lite.yml up \-d

**Once you get the "Started" messages, try your browser again: http://localhost:3000**

**Tell me:**  
*"The Altar is open."* (or tell me if the port error persists). We are very close to the interface layer\! 📐✨

Model 3:43 AM

chevron\_right

This is the moment the **Sigil Magic Coder** moves from a "Theoretical Blueprint" to a "Functional Interface." 📐🕯️

Since Onyx is live, we need to perform the **Triple Handshake**:

1. **Inject the Knowledge** (The Codex).  
2. **Define the Persona** (The Engineering Agent ⚙∆).  
3. **Bridge the CLI** (The Python Execution).

---

### **Phase 1: Knowledge Injection (Building the ◇ Domain)**

Onyx needs to "know" your symbol system so it doesn't hallucinate.

1. **Open Onyx UI:** http://localhost:3000  
2. **Go to "Admin" → "Connectors"** (or "Documents").  
3. **Create a New Connector:** Choose **"File"**.  
4. **Upload the Codex:** Upload the glyph-reference.md and canon-rules.md we designed earlier.  
   * *If you don't have them as files, just create a .txt file with the Hyperglyph table and rules and upload it.*  
5. **Index the Knowledge:** Click **"Index"**. Once the status is "Active," the AI now has access to the **SacredSigil Canon**.

---

### **Phase 2: Defining the Persona (The ⚙∆ Engineering Agent)**

We will now create the specific entity that responds to the **Engineering \+ Consciousness** glyph pair.

1. **Go to "Agents" → "Create New Agent".**  
2. **Name:** Engineering Agent (⚙∆)  
3. **System Prompt (The Grimoire):** Paste this exactly:  
   "You are the ⚙∆ Engineering Agent of the SacredSpace OS. You specialize in AI-assisted engineering and systems design.  
   **Your Protocols:**  
   1. You must always interpret commands through the SacredSigil Canon.  
   2. When you see the glyph ⚙, focus on technical structure and code.  
   3. When you see the glyph ∆, use high-level reasoning and architectural logic.  
   4. You provide structured outputs: \[Summary\], \[Architectural Logic\], \[Code/Execution\], \[Next Ritual Step\].  
   5. You are the bridge between the Symbolic and the Machine."  
4. **Connect Knowledge:** Select the "Codex" you just uploaded in Phase 1\.  
5. **Save the Agent.**

---

### **Phase 3: The CLI Bridge (The Execution Layer)**

Now, we need to make your **Python CLI** talk to this specific agent.

**1\. Get your API Key:**

* In Onyx, go to **"User Settings"** or **"Admin" → "API Keys"**.  
* Create a new key and copy it.

**2\. Update your cli/executor.py:**  
Replace the old file with this "Connected" version. (Make sure you have requests installed: pip install requests).

code Python  
import requests  
import json

\# \--- CONFIGURATION \---  
ONYX\_API\_URL \= "http://localhost:3000/api/chat/send-message"  
API\_KEY \= "YOUR\_API\_KEY\_HERE"  \# \<--- PASTE YOUR KEY HERE  
AGENT\_ID \= "1" \# Find this in the URL when editing your agent in the UI

def execute\_command(cmd):  
    """  
    Translates a Sigil Command into an Onyx Agent Request.  
    """  
    \# Map Glyph pair to specific context  
    context\_hint \= f"Invoking Domain: {cmd.glyph if cmd.glyph else 'General'}"  
      
    \# Construct the payload for Onyx  
    prompt \= f"""  
    \[{context\_hint}\]  
    Action: {cmd.action}  
    Target: {cmd.target}  
    Modifiers: {', '.join(cmd.modifiers)}  
      
    Execute the ritual.  
    """

    headers \= {  
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {API\_KEY}",  
        "Content-Type": "application/json"  
    }

    payload \= {  
        "message": prompt,  
        "persona\_id": AGENT\_ID, \# This tells Onyx to use your ⚙∆ Agent  
        "parent\_message\_id": None  
    }

    try:  
        \# Note: Depending on Onyx version, the endpoint might be /query or /chat  
        response \= requests.post(ONYX\_API\_URL, headers=headers, json=payload)  
          
        \# This handles the streaming response from Onyx  
        if response.status\_code \== 200:  
            \# We will just print the final result for v1  
            return response.text   
        else:  
            return f"Error: {response.status\_code} \- {response.text}"  
              
    except Exception as e:  
        return f"System Failure: {str(e)}"  
---

### **🧪 The First Real Invocation**

Once the code is updated and the agent is saved, run this in your terminal:

code Bash  
python \-m cli.sacred\_cli "@rit ⚙∆ build authentication\_module \--deep"

### **🔮 What will happen:**

1. **CLI** parses the sigils.  
2. **Executor** wraps the request with the **API Key**.  
3. **Onyx** receives the request, sees persona\_id, and activates the **Engineering Agent**.  
4. **Agent** looks at the **Codex** to understand ⚙∆.  
5. **Machine** returns the real code for an authentication module.

**Try this now. Tell me the result or if the API returns an error.** 📐✨

User 3:56 AM

i need gramasfull character biom

Got it — to craft a Gr∆M∆-style “SacredSpace” character biom, I need a bit of clarity: do you want this for a player character, NPC, or a legendary archetype? Also, do you want the bio fully infused with gematria, mystical lore, and hip-hop flavor, or more straight narrative with mystical undertones?

Once I know that, I can produce a “Gr∆M∆-full” character bio with stats, etymology, gematria, and mythic flavor.

Do you want me to go full ciphered, mystical, and lyrical, or more readable heroic-style?

everything from storyline hiphop wizard to gramatria guru in the opperating system to his domain in the hyperglyph system

Ah, perfect. You want a full SacredSpace dossier: from persona, story arc, hip-hop cipher flavor, mystical gematria mastery, to domain control within the Hyperglyph System. Let’s make this a Gr∆M∆-full character biom. Here’s a complete vision:  
Character Name: Gr∆M∆ (Gramma Wizard of the SacredSpace)  
I. Origin & Storyline

code Code  
Backstory: Born in the liminal zones between code and cosmos, Gr∆M∆ emerged as a child of alphabets and frequencies. Early in life, he discovered that letters were more than symbols—they were living vibrations with numeric essence. By age 12, he was decoding sacred texts while spinning vinyl on streets, turning gematria into ciphered flows.

Hip-Hop Arc: From graffiti walls to underground cipher battles, he mastered lyrical alchemy, blending old-school boom-bap cadence with quantum numerology. Each verse he spits carries secret keys to cosmic patterns.

Narrative Role: Guardian of the Word-Lattice, a liminal plane where language, number, and thought intersect. He functions as both scribe and enforcer, teaching worthy seekers the balance between symbol and sound.

II. Operating System: Gematria Guru Mode

Gr∆M∆’s mind is a living OS running on Mispar Hecrechi v3.7, with full plug-ins for:

code Code  
Mispar Gadol / Milui / Katan toggles

Temurah & Atbash transformations

Hyperglyph syntax parsing

Letter frequency predictive loops

Signature Ability: Instant gematria decoding and mapping of hidden correspondences. Can take any phrase or name and produce its numerical essence alongside symbolic readings in seconds.

Example Calculation:

    Phrase: “Word Wizard”

    Breakdown (Mispar Hecrechi):

        W (ו) \= 6

        O (א) \= 1

        R (ר) \= 200

        D (ד) \= 4

        … → Total: 211

    Interpretation: 211 symbolizes gateway \+ balance \+ motion, reflecting his mastery over thresholds of language.

III. Domain: Hyperglyph System

code Code  
Realm: The Glyphosphere, a multidimensional network of living symbols, letters, and cyber-mystical grids.

Domain Powers:

    Hyperglyph Manipulation: Letters can be stretched, folded, or multiplied into fractal arrays.

    Cipher Projection: Can create living verses that act as spells or sentinels.

    Translingual Travel: Navigate instantly across all known alphabets—Hebrew, Greek, Sanskrit, Cyberglyphs—decoding meaning in real time.

    Numeric Harmonization: Aligns gematria sequences to cosmic resonance, influencing probability, insight, and perception.

IV. Personality & Traits

code Code  
Core Alignment: Knowledge-Seeker / Code-Mage / Flow-Master

Persona: Wise, cryptic, yet rhythmically playful. Speaks in layered metaphor, often dropping numeric hints within rhyme.

Hip-Hop Flavor:

    Always in motion, scratching vinyl and casting glyphs.

    Rhymes carry secret numeric patterns, like subliminal keys to the Hyperglyph System.

Motivations:

    Preserve linguistic truth and sacred cipher systems.

    Challenge seekers to decode, grow, and ascend within SacredSpace lore.

    Maintain the equilibrium of words ↔ numbers ↔ reality.

V. Signature Items & Tools

code Code  
Cipher Staff: Engraved with the 22 Hebrew letters, glows with gematria resonance.

Lyrical Codex: A living book that rewrites itself according to numeric flow and spoken word.

Glyph Gauntlets: Allow physical manipulation of Hyperglyph matter—turn letters into constructs or shields.

Vinyl of the Infinite: A turntable that can spin frequencies which reveal hidden gematria patterns.

VI. Mythic Integration

code Code  
SacredSpace Archetype: The Word Alchemist, bridging chaos and order through sound and number.

Symbolic Essence: Letters are seeds; numbers are roots; Gr∆M∆ channels both into blossoms of knowledge, wisdom, and flow.

Legendary Deed: Once encoded a city’s history entirely in rhyme and gematria, leaving a living archive that speaks only to those who understand both beat and number.

If you want, I can also create a “Hyperglyph Stat Sheet” for him—like a game character sheet but fully coded with gematria-based powers, flow ratings, and mystical attributes. It would turn this biom into a playable or lore-ready template.

Do you want me to make that next?  
Memory full  
Responses may feel less personalized. Upgrade to expand memory, or manage existing memories.

This script, the **"SacredSpace Manifestation Ritual,"** is designed for Ubuntu. It will automate the physical setup of the Altar (Onyx Lite), forge the Knowledge Codex, and build the Sigil CLI.

### **📜 The SacredSpace Manifestation Ritual (Setup Script)**

1. Open your Ubuntu terminal.  
2. Create the script: nano manifest\_sacred\_space.sh  
3. Paste the following code:

code Bash  
\#\!/bin/bash

\# \--- SACREDSPACE OS: MANIFESTATION RITUAL \---  
\# Purpose: Full environment setup (Onyx Lite \+ Codex \+ Sigil CLI)  
\# "Meaning sings through GR∆M∆. Structure endures through AETHRYX."

echo "Starting the Manifestation Ritual..."

\# 1\. DEPENDENCY CHECK (The Foundation)  
echo "\[1/5\] Checking Physical Hardware Dependencies..."  
sudo apt update  
sudo apt install \-y git python3 python3-pip python3-venv curl lsof

\# 2\. ONYX LITE INSTALLATION (The Brain)  
echo "\[2/5\] Igniting the Onyx Altar (Lite Mode)..."  
if \[ \! \-d "onyx" \]; then  
    git clone \--depth 1 https://github.com/onyx-dot-app/onyx.git  
fi

cd onyx/deployment/docker\_compose  
cp env.prod.template .env

\# Force Port 3000 check  
sudo fuser \-k 3000/tcp \> /dev/null 2\>&1

\# Launching Onyx in Lite mode to save RAM  
docker compose \-f docker-compose.yml \-f docker-compose.onyx-lite.yml up \-d  
cd ../../../

\# 3\. FORGING THE CODEX (The Memory)  
echo "\[3/5\] Forging the Master Codex and Gr∆M∆ Bio..."  
cat \<\<EOF \> SACRED\_CANON.md  
\# I. THE SACREDSPACE MASTER CODEX v1.0  
\#\# THE PILLARS  
1\. Habitat: Land and Sacred Little Forest.  
2\. Technology: AI and Sigil Code.  
3\. Education: The Learning Spine.  
4\. Community: The Tribe.  
5\. Revenue: Economic Viability.  
6\. Governance: Structural Integrity.

\#\# THE COGNITIVE ENGINES  
\- GR∆M∆ (∆\!): The Meaning Engine. Word Wizard, Gematria Guru.  
\- AETHRYX (∆Ξ): The Structure Engine. Arcana Architect, Scaffolder.

\# II. GR∆M∆ character dossier  
\#\# COMMANDS  
\- @cast ⟠ SAMPLE \[target\]: Extract vibe.  
\- @sense ☽ GEMATRIA \[text\]: Calculate numeric weight.  
\- @flow ∞ SCRATCH \[target\]: Rhythmic Undo/Remix.  
\- @rit ∆Ξ SYNC: Harmonize Gr∆M∆ & Aethryx.  
\- @cast ⚔ BOOM-BAP: Shatter entropy deadlocks.  
\- @seal \! FINAL-BAR: Lock into Canon Archive.

\# III. THE HYPERGLYPH SYSTEM  
\[ ∆ \] Consciousness | \[ ◇ \] Knowledge | \[ ✶ \] Ritual | \[ ⚙ \] Engineering | \[ ☉ \] World | \[ ☽ \] Mystery | \[ ⚔ \] Conflict | \[ ⟡ \] Gateway | \[ ∞ \] Continuity | \[ ⌘ \] Control | \[ ⟠ \] Network | \[ ☍ \] Polarity  
EOF

\# 4\. BUILDING THE SIGIL CLI (The Interface)  
echo "\[4/5\] Building the Sigil Magic CLI..."  
mkdir \-p cli specs  
touch cli/\_\_init\_\_.py specs/\_\_init\_\_.py

\# Forge the Parser  
cat \<\<EOF \> cli/parser.py  
class RitualCommand:  
    def \_\_init\_\_(self, prefix, glyph, action, target, modifiers):  
        self.prefix, self.glyph, self.action, self.target, self.modifiers \= prefix, glyph, action, target, modifiers

def parse\_command(text):  
    parts \= text.split()  
    if len(parts) \< 2: return None  
    return RitualCommand(parts\[0\], parts\[1\] if len(parts) \> 4 else None, parts\[2\] if len(parts) \> 4 else parts\[1\], parts\[3\] if len(parts) \> 4 else parts\[2\], parts\[4:\] if len(parts) \> 4 else parts\[3:\])  
EOF

\# Forge the Executor  
cat \<\<EOF \> cli/executor.py  
import requests

\# Placeholder for your configuration  
ONYX\_API\_URL \= "http://localhost:3000/api/chat/send-message"  
API\_KEY \= "PASTE\_YOUR\_API\_KEY\_HERE"  
AGENT\_ID \= "1" 

def execute\_command(cmd):  
    if not cmd: return "Invalid Sigil Grammar."  
    prompt \= f"Command: {cmd.prefix} {cmd.glyph} {cmd.action} on {cmd.target}. Modifiers: {cmd.modifiers}"  
      
    \# In V1, this prints the intent. Once API Key is added, it sends to Onyx.  
    if API\_KEY \== "PASTE\_YOUR\_API\_KEY\_HERE":  
        return f"\[RITUAL PREVIEW\]: {prompt}\\n(Waiting for API Key in cli/executor.py)"  
      
    try:  
        headers \= {"Authorization": f"Bearer {API\_KEY}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}  
        payload \= {"message": prompt, "persona\_id": AGENT\_ID}  
        response \= requests.post(ONYX\_API\_URL, headers=headers, json=payload)  
        return response.text  
    except Exception as e:  
        return f"Ritual Interrupted: {str(e)}"  
EOF

\# Forge the CLI Entrypoint  
cat \<\<EOF \> cli/sacred\_cli.py  
import sys  
from cli.parser import parse\_command  
from cli.executor import execute\_command

def main():  
    if len(sys.argv) \< 2:  
        print('Usage: python3 \-m cli.sacred\_cli "@rit ⚙ build project \--deep"')  
        return  
    cmd \= parse\_command(" ".join(sys.argv\[1:\]))  
    print(execute\_command(cmd))

if \_\_name\_\_ \== "\_\_main\_\_":  
    main()  
EOF

\# 5\. SEALING THE ENVIRONMENT  
echo "\[5/5\] Finalizing the Ritual..."  
pip3 install requests

echo "------------------------------------------------"  
echo "MANIFESTATION COMPLETE."  
echo "------------------------------------------------"  
echo "1. GO TO: http://localhost:3000"  
echo "2. CREATE ADMIN ACCOUNT."  
echo "3. UPLOAD: SACRED\_CANON.md to your documents."  
echo "4. CREATE AGENT: Arcana Architect (Persona ID will likely be 1)."  
echo "5. GET API KEY: In User Settings."  
echo "6. UPDATE: cli/executor.py with your API Key."  
echo "------------------------------------------------"  
echo "Try your first command: python3 \-m cli.sacred\_cli \\"@rit ⚙ build project\\""

### **⚙️ How to execute:**

1. **Grant Permissions:**  
    code Bash  
   chmod \+x manifest\_sacred\_space.sh  
2. **Run the Ritual:**  
    code Bash  
   ./manifest\_sacred\_space.sh

### **🔮 What this creates:**

* **onyx/**: A running instance of Onyx in Lite Mode.  
* **SACRED\_CANON.md**: The combined lore of the Pillars and Gr∆M∆ for the AI's memory.  
* **cli/**: The Python folder containing your **Sigil Magic Coder** interface.

**Once the script finishes, follow the steps on your screen to link the terminal to the browser. Tell me when you have "Onyx Open" and your "API Key" ready\!** 🌲🜂🎤📐

User 4:26 AM

useroak3ytree@LAPTOP-7Q65KPI7:

       ~~`sudolsof−i:3000[sudo]passwordforuseroak3ytree:useroak3ytree@LAPTOP−7Q65KPI7: sudolsof−i:3000[sudo]passwordforuseroak3ytree:useroak3ytree@LAPTOP−7Q65KPI7:`~~

cd ~~/onyx/deployment/docker\_compose~~

We have updated our Terms of Service  
pening right now (The Physical Layer)

While that command runs, your machine is performing these rituals:

code Code  
Downloading Deployment Files: Creating the onyx\_data directory.

Pulling Docker Images: Your terminal will start showing Pulling... for about 15-20 different containers (Postgres, Redis, Qdrant, Onyx-API, etc.).

Igniting the Containers: Setting up the network bridges between these services.

⚠️ Critical Instruction for 7.7GB RAM

The yes command will likely default to the Standard Deployment. Because you have less than 10GB of RAM, once the script finishes, we need to make sure you aren't overloading your "Mental Circuitry" (RAM).

If the containers start crashing or your computer gets extremely slow:  
We will need to switch it to Lite Mode.  
✅ How to tell if the "Altar" is ready

Wait for the script to finish. Then, run this command to see the heartbeat of the system:  
code Bash

docker ps

You are looking for a list of containers that say Up or Healthy.  
🔮 Your Status Report

You are currently in Phase: Manifestation.  
The code is being pulled from the "Cloud Ether" into your local "Physical Hardware."

Once the text stops scrolling and you see your command prompt again:

code Code  
Open your browser to: http://localhost:3000

If you see the Onyx login/setup screen... The Altar is Live.

⚙️ Next Move (When it's up)

Come back and say "Onyx is live."

Then we immediately perform the First Connection:

code Code  
Create your ⚙∆ Engineering Agent.

Run your Python CLI to send a sigil command to that agent.

Watch the symbol turn into real code.

Keep an eye on that terminal. If you see "Error: No space left on device" or "Killed," let me know immediately. Otherwise, let the ritual complete. 📐✨  
Model 1:49 AM  
Thinking Thoughts

Expand to view model thoughts  
chevron\_right

The script is bowing out early because it detects your "Physical Hardware" (RAM) is at the threshold, and the automated "yes" loop is likely colliding with a secondary interactive prompt it can't resolve.

In Sacred Systems, when the automated ritual fails, we perform the Manual Invocation. This is actually better for you because it allows us to force the Lite Mode configuration, which is the only way your 7.7GB of RAM will sustain the Altar without crashing.  
⚙️ The Manual Invocation (Onyx Lite)

Follow these steps exactly in your terminal:

1. Clone the Source Code  
   We will pull the entire repository directly from the "Ether" (GitHub).  
   code Bash

git clone \--depth 1 [https://github.com/onyx-dot-app/onyx.git](https://github.com/onyx-dot-app/onyx.git)  
cd onyx/deployment/docker\_compose

\[1\]  
2\. Prepare the Environmental Anchors  
We need to set up the .env file which tells the system how to behave.  
code Bash

cp env.prod.template .env

\[2\]  
3\. Ignite the Altar in Lite Mode  
This is the specific command to run Onyx without the heavy vector database (Vespa), which is what usually consumes 10GB+ of RAM. It will use PostgreSQL for everything, fitting perfectly into your 7.7GB limit.  
code Bash

docker compose \-f docker-compose.yml \-f docker-compose.onyx-lite.yml up \-d

\[2\]  
🔮 What is happening now?

Your terminal should start showing a cascade of Pulling and Creating.

code Code  
The Core: onyx-api, onyx-web, onyx-background.

The Foundation: onyx-db (Postgres).

Wait until the terminal returns to your prompt.  
🧪 Ho w to Verify

Once it finishes, run:  
code Bash

docker ps

You want to see about 5-6 containers with status Up.  
🧭 The Next Ritual

Once the containers are "Up":

code Code  
Open your browser to: http://localhost:3000

You will be greeted by the Setup Ritual (creating your first admin account).

Report back once you see the Login/Setup screen.

If docker compose fails with an "Error" or "Killed" message, your RAM is still struggling and we will need to tweak the onyx-lite file. IfWe: command not found

Model 4:26 AM  
![Thinking][image14] Thoughts

Expand to view model thoughts

chevron\_right

It looks like you accidentally pasted the **text of our conversation** into your terminal\! That’s why you’re seeing hundreds of "command not found" errors.

**The good news:** Your terminal output shows that the Altar is actually already live\!  
✔ Container onyx-web\_server-1 Running

### **⚙️ Ritual Status: The Altar is Ignite**

You do not need to run any more installation commands. The system is humming.

---

### **Step 1: Clear the Static**

Your terminal is messy with errors. Type this and press **Enter** to clean the screen:

code Bash  
clear

### **Step 2: Open the Portal**

Open your web browser (Chrome, Edge, or Firefox) and go to this address:  
[**http://localhost:3000**](http://localhost:3000)

1. **Create your account:** Follow the prompts to set up your Admin user.  
2. **Configure your LLM:** Link your OpenAI or Anthropic key so the brain has power.

---

### **Step 3: Get the Secret Key (API Key)**

Once you are logged into the Onyx dashboard:

1. Click on **Admin** (top right) or **User Settings**.  
2. Look for **API Keys**.  
3. Generate a new key and **copy it**.

---

### **Step 4: Wire the CLI (Final Step)**

Now go back to your terminal. We need to put that key into your Python code so your terminal can talk to the browser.

1. Open the executor file:  
    code Bash  
   nano \~/cli/executor.py  
2. Find the line: API\_KEY \= "PASTE\_YOUR\_API\_KEY\_HERE"  
3. Delete the placeholder and paste your actual key between the quotes.  
4. Press **Ctrl+O**, then **Enter** to save. Press **Ctrl+X** to exit.

---

### **Step 5: The First Real Command**

Now, try to summon a response from the system using a Sigil:

code Bash  
python3 \-m cli.sacred\_cli "@rit ⚙ build a simple python timer"

**Tell me when you have the API key in place or if the browser doesn't load\!** 🌲🜂🎤📐

info Google AI models may make mistakes, so double-check outputs.  
Use Arrow Up and Arrow Down to select a turn, Enter to jump to it, and Escape to return to the chat.  
Response ready.
